title: Circuit-Energy Prior Features
summary: Public tyre-model references emphasize that circuit energy/severity changes degradation shape and viable stint length by track.
rationale: Public tyre-model references emphasize that circuit energy/severity changes degradation shape and viable stint length by track. Your current heuristics use fixed compound life values, which likely miss race-specific variation between low-energy venues and high-degradation tracks.
implementation_hint: Add a small manually curated `Race -> track_energy_bin` map from public F1 tyre-severity descriptions, then interact it with `Compound`, `TyreLife`, `Cumulative_Degradation`, and estimated laps remaining. Useful features: severity-adjusted expected stint life, severity-adjusted excess wear, and severity-specific late-race stop margins.
expected_effect: More transferable stint-window features than one global compound-life table, especially across races with different abrasion profiles.
risk: External mapping is coarse and partly subjective; a bad map can just duplicate race identity with extra noise.
